docs.title: Multimodal Agent
docs.text:
A lightweight, production-ready wrapper around Google Gemini with:

  • Multimodal support (text + images)
  • Session-based conversational memory
  • SQLite-powered RAG vector store
  • High test coverage (90%+)
  • Robust CLI (agent)
  • Retry logic + error handling
  • Extensible architecture for plugins & tooling

This documentation will guide you through:

docs.title: Getting Started
docs.text:
• Installation
  • CLI Usage
  • Python API

docs.title: Core System
docs.text:
• Architecture Overview
  • Sessions
  • RAG Memory
  • History Management

docs.title: Advanced Features
docs.text:
• Response Metadata Schema

  • JSON response mode

  • Token Usage Logging
  • Chunk Normalization (Planned)

docs.title: Development
docs.text:
• Project structure
  • Running tests
  • Coverage reports
  • Contributing

docs.title: Version Highlights (v0.2.6)
docs.text:
• Added SQLite-backed RAG store
  • Added session-based memory
  • Added agent history commands
  • Updated schema for future usage
  • New docs for RAG and session management

docs.title: Why This Project Exists
docs.text:
This agent sits between Gemini and your applications to provide:

  • A clean interface
  • Safer, predictable behavior
  • Local memory without external services
  • Debuggable pipelines

docs.title: 🚀 Quick Start
docs.text:
Install:

pip install multimodal-agent

Ask a question:

agent ask "What is recursion?"

Start a chat session:

agent chat --session my_chat

Enable verbose metadata:

agent ask "hello" --verbose

docs.title: Need help?
docs.text: Open an issue on GitHub or submit a pull request. PRs improving docs, tests, or performance are always welcome.

docs.title: The Agent Class
docs.text:
multimodal_agent.agent_core.MultiModalAgent

The main class powering multimodal communication with Gemini.

docs.title: Initialization
docs.text:
from multimodal_agent.agent_core import MultiModalAgent

agent = MultiModalAgent(model="gemini-2.5-flash")
Arguments: | Arg | Type | Default | Description | | ---- | ---- | ------- | ----------------- | | model | str | "gemini-2.5-flash" | Gemini model name | | api_version | str | "v1" | Gemini API version | | client | genai.Client | None | Inject custom client (used for testing)|

docs.title: ask(prompt: str) -> str
docs.text:
Sends a text-only request.

agent.ask("What is recursion?")

docs.title: ask_with_image(prompt: str, image: Part) -> str
docs.text:
Sends an image + text multimodal prompt.

image = load_image_as_part("photo.jpg")
agent.ask_with_image("Describe this image.", image)

docs.title: chat()
docs.text:
Starts a REPL (interactive loop) with memory persistence.

agent chat

docs.title: Retry Logic
docs.text:
All request methods use:

safe_generate_content()

Which includes:

  • Exponential backoff

  • Retryable errors (503 overload)

  • RetryableError and NonRetryableError exceptions

docs.title: Exceptions Raised
docs.text:
• RetryableError

  • NonRetryableError

  • AgentError (base class)

docs.title: Chunk Normalization
docs.text:
Chunk size normalization aims to keep embeddings:

  • smaller
  • more searchable
  • less noisy
  • cheaper (fewer tokens)

docs.title: Why Normalize?
docs.text:
You don't want:

  • giant paragraphs embedded directly
  • noisy logs going into RAG
  • 3000-token embeddings for a simple chat

docs.title: Planned Behaviors
docs.text:
1. Split long text into semantic units
  2. Strip noise (timestamps, logs, repeated content)
  3. De-duplicate similar chunks
  4. Add chunk metadata

docs.title: Command Line Interface for Multimodal Agent
docs.text:
The agent CLI provides a clean, lightweight way to interact with Google Gemini using:

  • Text input
  • Image + text multimodal input
  • Session-based chat
  • RAG-powered history retrieval
  • Memory management commands
  • Optional verbose metadata output

This page documents all CLI commands for version 0.2.6+ .

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
Once installed:

pip install multimodal-agent

Run:

agent --help

docs.title: Ask a text question
docs.text: agent ask "Explain quantum tunneling"

docs.title: Options:
docs.text: Flag Description --session id Use (or create) a session ID, with RAG memory --no-rag Disable retrieval --model model_name Override Gemini model --verbose Include metadata fields (tokens, timings, chunks)

docs.title: Example:
docs.text: agent ask "What are embeddings?" --session research1 --verbose

docs.title: JSON output
docs.text: agent ask "hello" --json

docs.title: Ask with an image
docs.text: agent image cat.jpg "Describe this image"

docs.title: Example:
docs.text: agent image report.png "Summarize this chart" --session analytics

docs.title: Interactive Chat
docs.text:
agent chat

Start a session-bound chat:

agent chat --session my_chat

While chatting:

  • Your prompts are stored as “chunks”
  • Embeddings are added automatically
  • RAG retrieves relevant past messages

Exit with:

exit

docs.title: Show most recent messages
docs.text:
agent history show

Optional:

agent history show --session s1 --limit 20

docs.title: Delete a specific entry
docs.text:
agent history delete3

Deletes by chunk ID .

docs.title: Reset all history
docs.text:
⚠️ Deletes sessions + chunks + embeddings:

agent historyclear

docs.title: Summarize history using LLM
docs.text:
agent history summary

with session filter:

agent history summary--session s1

This uses the internal agent:

  • Loads N recent chunks
  • Converts them to summary prompt
  • Outputs AI-generated summary

docs.title: Verbose Mode (--verbose)
docs.text:
Every content generation command supports:

--verbose

This prints:

{
  "answer": "...",
  "metadata": {
    "tokens": {...},
    "rag_used": true,
    "session_id": "s1",
    "retrieved_chunks": [
      {"id": 3, "score": 0.81, "preview": "previous message"}
    ]
  }
}

Verbose mode is designed to be machine-readable , powering:

  • Flutter extensions
  • VS Code extensions
  • CLI debugging tools
  • Model introspection tools

docs.title: Global Flags
docs.text:
These flags work with every command:

Flag Description --model Set Gemini model (default: gemini-2.5-flash) --session Use/create a named session --no-rag Disable RAG pipeline --verbose Output metadata schema --debug Enable debug logging --version Show version

docs.title: Testing the CLI
docs.text:
To run CLI tests:

pytest tests/test_cli.py -q

To run tests + coverage:

pytest --cov

docs.title: Future CLI Enhancements
docs.text:
• Token usage logging
  • Chunk-size normalization
  • Local tool-calling plugins
  • Async CLI via AsyncMultiModalAgent
  • Project scaffolding for Flutter + Gemini

docs.title: Error Handling
docs.text: The agent defines structured custom exceptions:

docs.title: AgentError
docs.text: Base class for all custom agent exceptions.

docs.title: RetryableError
docs.text:
Raised when:

  • Gemini returns HTTP 503
  • Model is overloaded
  • Retries are exhausted
  • Exponential backoff completes without success

docs.title: NonRetryableError
docs.text:
Raised when:

  • The model throws a non-retryable exception
  • Unexpected errors occur in the API call

docs.title: InvalidImageError
docs.text:
Raised by:

  • load_image_as_part()
  • Bad paths
  • Unsupported formats
  • PIL decoding failures 
from multimodal_agent.utils import load_image_as_part
from multimodal_agent.errors import InvalidImageError

try:
    img = load_image_as_part("bad.png")
except InvalidImageError as e:
    print("Could not load image:", e)

docs.title: Simple Text Request
docs.text:
from multimodal_agent.agent_core import MultiModalAgent

agent = MultiModalAgent()
print(agent.ask("hello"))

docs.title: Custom Model
docs.text: agent --model gemini-pro ask "summarize transformers"

docs.title: Multimodal Request
docs.text:
from multimodal_agent.utils import load_image_as_part
from multimodal_agent.agent_core import MultiModalAgent

agent = MultiModalAgent()
img = load_image_as_part("cat.jpg")

print(agent.ask_with_image("describe this", img))

docs.title: Error Handling Example
docs.text:
from multimodal_agent.agent_core import MultiModalAgent
from multimodal_agent.errors import AgentError

agent = MultiModalAgent()

try:
    print(agent.ask("hello"))
except AgentError as e:
    print("The request failed:", e)

docs.title: History & Memory Management
docs.text:
The CLI includes ergonomic tools for inspecting and managing memory.

Introduced in v0.2.6.

docs.title: Show history
docs.text: agent history show --limit 20

docs.title: Show history for a session
docs.text: agent history show --session chatA

docs.title: Delete a specific chunk
docs.text: agent history delete 42

docs.title: Clear history
docs.text:
agent history clear

or per session: 

agent history clear --session abc

docs.title: Summaries
docs.text:
agent history summary --limit 50

The agent summarizes recent history using Gemini (RAG disabled for the summary call).

docs.title: Under the Hood
docs.text:
• Uses the SQLiteRAGStore
  • Performs fast range queries via indexed tables
  • Deletes cascade into embeddings

docs.title: Install from PyPI (recommended)
docs.text: pip install multimodal-agent

docs.title: Install from source
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/horam/multimodal-agent.git
cd multimodal-agent
pip install -e .[dev,test]

docs.title: Environment Variables
docs.text:
Create a .env file in the project root:

GOOGLE_API_KEY=your_api_key
LOGLEVEL=INFO
The CLI automatically loads:

ROOT/.env
This file is intentionally excluded by .gitignore.

docs.title: Verify installation
docs.text:
agent --version
If the command runs successfully, the installation is complete.

docs.title: JSON Response Mode
docs.text:
Since v0.3.0, Multimodal-Agent supports structured JSON output via:

agent.ask(question, response_format="json")

docs.title: How it works
docs.text:
The agent instructs the model to return raw JSON without backticks.

  • The final result is always an AgentResponse object.

  • Parsed JSON is stored in response.data (a Python dict or None).

  • The raw text returned by the model is always accessible via response.text.

  • Markdown fences such as:

{ ... }
are automatically stripped during parsing.

  • Invalid or malformed JSON cleanly falls back to:

{"raw": "<original text>"}
This ensures JSON mode never crashes.

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
from multimodal_agent import MultiModalAgent

agent = MultiModalAgent(enable_rag=False)

result = agent.ask(
    "Return a movie object with title and year.",
    response_format="json",
)

print(result.data)   # parsed dict
print(result.text)   # raw text from the model
Output:

{"title": "The Matrix", "year": 1999}

docs.title: JSON from Images
docs.text:
from multimodal_agent import MultiModalAgent
from multimodal_agent.utils import load_image_as_part

agent = MultiModalAgent(enable_rag=False)
img = load_image_as_part("cat.jpg")

result = agent.ask_with_image(
    "Describe this image as JSON.",
    img,
    response_format="json",
)

print(result.data)

docs.title: Offline Mode
docs.text:
If no GOOGLE_API_KEY is set:

export GOOGLE_API_KEY=""
The agent switches to FakeResponse simulation mode.

JSON mode still returns an AgentResponse, and .data becomes:

result = agent.ask("hello", response_format="json")
print(result.data)
Output:

{"raw": "FAKE_RESPONSE: hello"}
JSON mode behaves consistently across online and offline scenarios.

docs.title: Logging
docs.text: The package uses a unified logging utility:

docs.title: Features
docs.text:
• Consistent formatting
  • Output to stdout
  • Prevents duplicate handlers
  • Respects LOGLEVEL environment variable
  • Works with pytest caplog

docs.title: Changing Log Level
docs.text:
In .env:

LOGLEVEL=DEBUG

Or via CLI:

agent --debug ask "hello"

docs.title: Memory System
docs.text:
The agent stores conversation turns in:

~/.multimodal_agent/history.json

docs.title: Functions
docs.text:
• load_memory()

  • save_memory(list)

  • append_memory(entry)

  • delete_memory_index(i)

  • reset_memory()

  • summarize_memory()

Memory is plain JSON — easy to inspect and portable.

docs.title: Response Metadata Schema
docs.text:
The CLI supports two response formats:

  • Minimal mode (default)
  • Verbose mode (--verbose)

This keeps the UX clean while allowing programmatic access to details.

docs.title: Minimal Schema (default)
docs.text:
{
  "text": "Hello!",
  "image": null
}
Purpose:

  • Clean CLI output

  • Human-friendly

  • Great for normal usage

docs.title: Verbose Schema (--verbose)
docs.text:
{
  "text": "Hello!",
  "meta": {
    "model": "gemini-2.5-flash",
    "tokens": {
      "prompt": 128,
      "completion": 42,
      "total": 170
    },
    "latency_ms": 89,
    "rag": {
      "chunks_used": [
        { "id": 3, "score": 0.92 },
        { "id": 8, "score": 0.88 }
      ]
    }
  }
}
Includes:

  • Model name

  • Token usage

  • Latency

docs.title: RAG chunk retrieval info
docs.text:
Recommendation

Default = minimal

Power users = add --verbose

docs.title: Quickstart
docs.text: Below are the minimum examples needed to use the agent.

docs.title: Text Generation
docs.text:
from multimodal_agent import MultiModalAgent

agent = MultiModalAgent()
resp = agent.ask("Tell me something interesting about the universe.")
print(resp.text)

docs.title: Image + Text Generation
docs.text:
from multimodal_agent import MultiModalAgent
from multimodal_agent.utils import load_image_as_part

agent = MultiModalAgent()

image = load_image_as_part("cat.jpg")
resp = agent.ask_with_image("Describe this cat.", image)

print(resp.text)

docs.title: CLI Quickstart
docs.text:
agent ask "hello world"
agent image photo.jpg "what do you see?"
agent chat
agent --version
agent --debug ask "hello"

docs.title: Error Handling Example
docs.text:
from multimodal_agent import MultiModalAgent
from multimodal_agent.errors import AgentError

agent = MultiModalAgent()

try:
    print(agent.ask("hello").text)
except AgentError as e:
    print("The request failed:", e)

docs.title: JSON Response Mode (v0.3.0)
docs.text:
The agent can return structured JSON:

from multimodal_agent import MultiModalAgent

agent = MultiModalAgent(enable_rag=False)

resp = agent.ask(
    "Return a JSON person object.",
    response_format="json"
)

print(resp.data)  # → Python dict

Supported behavior

  • Accepts raw JSON responses

  • Removes ```json fenced blocks

  • Fallback to {"raw": "..."}

  • Works identically in offline FakeResponse mode

  • Returns an AgentResponse with: 

    resp.text  # original text
resp.data  # parsed JSON dict
resp.usage # token usage (dict or None)

docs.title: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) in Multimodal Agent (0.2.6+)
docs.text:
RAG enhances your agent by giving it persistent memory , context retrieval , and long-range recall across sessions.

This document explains:

  • What RAG is
  • How it works inside multimodal_agent
  • The embedding pipeline
  • Storage layout (SQLite)
  • Retrieval algorithm
  • How to enable/disable it
  • Testing strategy

docs.title: What is RAG?
docs.text:
RAG = Retrieval-Augmented Generation

Your messages are:

  1. Stored as chunks
  2. Embedded into numerical vectors
  3. Retrieved based on similarity when answering new prompts

This allows the model to recall:

  • Prior discussions
  • Long-running chats
  • Multi-step reasoning
  • Project-specific information
  • Custom rules or preferences

RAG dramatically improves coherence and personalization.

docs.title: How RAG Works in Multimodal Agent
docs.text:
The full RAG pipeline:

USER MESSAGE
    ↓
Store as chunk → Embed → Save vector
    ↓
Retrieve similar chunks (KNN search)
    ↓
Construct augmented prompt
    ↓
Generate final answer using Google Gemini

docs.title: Chunk Structure
docs.text:
Every message (user + agent) becomes a chunk :

{
  "id":42,
  "session_id":"s1",
  "role":"user",
  "content":"Explain reactive power",
  "source":"chat",
  "created_at":"2024-02-01T12:22:11"
}

Why chunks?

  • Separates text from metadata
  • Enables easy indexing & filtering
  • Plays nicely with embeddings

docs.title: Embedding Pipeline
docs.text:
When a chunk is added:

content → embedding model → [vector] → store in “embeddings” table

Embedding definition:

{
  "chunk_id":42,
  "model":"text-embedding-004",
  "dim":768,
  "embedding":"[0.12, -0.55, ...]"
}

Your implementation ensures:

  • deterministic embedding calls
  • re-embedding only if missing
  • composable design for custom models later

docs.title: Storage Backend (SQLite)
docs.text: Multimodal Agent uses a lightweight, portable SQLite DB:

docs.title: sessions
docs.text: Tracks all chat threads.

docs.title: chunks
docs.text: Stores every message.

docs.title: embeddings
docs.text:
Stores numeric vectors for similarity search.

🎯 Reasons for SQLite:

  • Zero dependencies
  • Easy to bundle with Flutter extensions
  • Perfect for local/offline memory
  • Very fast for small-to-medium RAG workloads

docs.title: Retrieval Algorithm (Similarity Search)
docs.text: When a new question arrives:

docs.title: Step 1 — Embed the query text
docs.text: (same embedding model as chunks)

docs.title: Step 2 — Retrieve top-k similar chunks
docs.text:
SELECT chunk_id, embedding FROM embeddings
WHERE model = ?

docs.title: Step 3 — Compute cosine similarity
docs.text: score = dot(query, emb) / (norm(query) * norm(emb))

docs.title: Step 4 — Return top results
docs.text:
Your implementation returns:

[(similarity_score, Chunk), …]

docs.title: Step 5 — Build augmented prompt
docs.text:
[SUMMARYOF RELEVANT HISTORY]
User: 



This augmentation is  transparent and automatic .

docs.title: Why RAG Matters for This Project
docs.text:
Your agent becomes:

  • More personal
  • More helpful over time
  • Better at tracking context
  • Able to recall long, multi-step conversations
  • Capable of advanced workflows (research, writing, debugging)

And in the future:

RAG will power your Flutter plugin for:

  • Project-wide recall
  • Natural-language code generation
  • Memory inside dev tools & IDEs

docs.title: Testing Strategy
docs.text:
RAG code is isolated behind:

SQLiteRAGStore

Two modes are used in tests:

docs.title: Fake store (default)
docs.text:
Used for all non-RAG tests

Ensures stability and speed

Prevents accidental DB operations

docs.title: Real store (@pytest.mark.use_real_rag)
docs.text:
Tests database logic & embed/search flow

Your conftest.py handles this automatically.

docs.title: RAG enabled by default
docs.text: agent ask "hello"

docs.title: Disable RAG
docs.text: agent ask "hello" --no-rag

docs.title: Session-specific RAG
docs.text:
agent chat --session research2025
agent history summary--session research2025

docs.title: RAG Limitations
docs.text:
• No chunk-size normalization yet (coming next)
  • Embeddings are stored for full messages; splitting not implemented
  • No embedding caching between models
  • No multi-model index
  • No hybrid search (BM25 + embeddings)

These are planned improvements.

docs.title: RAG Details
docs.text:
• Memory stored at ~/.multimodal_agent/memory.db
  • Each message is chunked
  • Embeddings stored per chunk
  • Cosine similarity search
  • Used automatically during ask/chat

Tables:

  • sessions
  • chunks
  • embeddings

Search:

rag_store.search_similar(query="hello", model="text-embedding-004")

docs.title: 0.2.6 — RAG v1
docs.text:
• Chunk storage
  • Embedding pipeline
  • KNN search
  • Session integration
  • Summaries and history tools
  • Tests for all parts

docs.title: 0.2.7 — Chunk Normalization
docs.text:
• Split long messages into manageable chunks
  • Normalize to N characters
  • Add chunk headers
  • Improve recall accuracy

docs.title: 0.2.8 — Query Expansion
docs.text:
• Use model to rewrite user queries
  • Improve retrieval

docs.title: 0.3.x — Embedding Cache
docs.text:
• DB-level dedupe
  • Hash-based cache

docs.title: 0.4.x — Hybrid Search
docs.text: • BM25 + embeddings combined

docs.title: Summary
docs.text:
RAG transforms your agent from a basic API wrapper into a stateful, context-aware assistant with:

  • Memory
  • Recall
  • Context stitching
  • Session isolation
  • Accurate and meaningful retrieval

This document gives developers full insight into how to extend or integrate RAG with future tools (including the upcoming Flutter extension).

docs.title: Server Mode (v0.5.0)
docs.text:
Start server:

agent server
Base URL:

http://127.0.0.1:8000
Supports:

  • /ask

  • /ask_with_image

  • /generate

  • /memory/search

docs.title: How sessions work in Multimodal Agent (0.2.6+)
docs.text:
Sessions allow you to run multiple isolated or parallel conversations using:

  • Session-scoped memory
  • RAG-based retrieval
  • History separation
  • Multi-tab CLI workflows (e.g., research1, bugfix2, article-draft)

This document explains how sessions work internally and how to use them in the CLI & Python API.

docs.title: What is a Session?
docs.text:
A session is simply an ID (string) associated with:

  • All messages you send in that session
  • All agent responses
  • All embeddings generated for those messages

This lets you have:

  • Long conversations without mixing contexts
  • Multiple independent threads (e.g., project_a , r1_research , draft3 )
  • Fine-grained RAG memory filtering

docs.title: 1. When you pass:
docs.text:
--session s1

The agent automatically:

  1. Creates the session if it doesn't exist
  2. Stores all chunks in the DB with session_id = "s1"
  3. Embeds each message (both user + agent)
  4. Performs RAG retrieval using:
SELECT * FROM embeddings 
WHERE model =  AND session_id = "s1"
ORDERBY cosine_similarity DESC



If --session is  not used , the messages belong to:


session_id = "default"

docs.title: RAG + Sessions
docs.text:
With RAG enabled (default):

  • RAG searches within the same session , if session is set
  • Or across all sessions if no session is provided
  • Retrieval is filtered by model and chunk role
  • The agent constructs a recap-context from top-K chunks
  • This recap is inserted before your prompt

This improves answer quality in ongoing conversations without polluting unrelated ones.

docs.title: Start a new session:
docs.text: agent ask "hello"--session my_session

docs.title: Start an isolated chat:
docs.text: agent chat --session story_draft

docs.title: List session history:
docs.text: agent history show--session story_draft

docs.title: Summarize session memory:
docs.text: agent history summary--session story_draft

docs.title: Delete a session (indirectly, via chunks):
docs.text: agent historyclear

docs.title: Basic example:
docs.text:
from multimodal_agent import MultiModalAgent

agent = MultiModalAgent()

resp = agent.ask(
    "Summarize this topic",
    session_id="research2",
)
print(resp)

docs.title: With image:
docs.text:
from multimodal_agent.utils import load_image_as_part

img = load_image_as_part("chart.png")

resp = agent.ask_with_image(
    "Explain this chart",
    img,
    session_id="analysis_2025"
)

docs.title: Chat loop with persisted session:
docs.text: agent.chat(session_id="long_chat")

docs.title: Database Structure
docs.text: Sessions live in the SQLite store:

docs.title: sessions table
docs.text:
Column
Type
Description




id
TEXT PK
Session ID


label
TEXT
Optional label for UI tools


created_at
TIMESTAMP
Auto timestamp

docs.title: chunks table
docs.text:
Each message (user OR agent):

Column Description id Auto PK session_id FK → sessions role user / agent content Text content source chat / ask / image created_at Auto timestamp

docs.title: embeddings table
docs.text:
Each chunk is embedded once:

Column Description chunk_id FK → chunks model embedding model name dim vector dimension embedding JSON vector

docs.title: Testing Sessions
docs.text:
Tests that expect real RAG behavior use:

@pytest.mark.use_real_rag

Fake store is used automatically for others.

docs.title: Recommended Session Strategy
docs.text: Use Case Suggested Session Debugging --session debug Research notes --session research_topic Multi-doc summarization --session docset_2025 Teaching agent custom rules --session persona1 Story writing --session novel_draft1

docs.title: Tips
docs.text:
• Use sessions to separate unrelated conversations .
  • RAG becomes significantly more accurate when sessions isolate context.
  • You can maintain long-running threads like career, health, side_project.

docs.title: Token Usage Logging (Planned)
docs.text: Token accounting is crucial for: - cost tracking - optimization - debugging latency - improving your extension/tooling

docs.title: CLI Support
docs.text:
When using:

agent ask "hello" --verbose

You will see:

"tokens": {
  "prompt": 54,
  "completion": 18,
  "total": 72
}

docs.title: Application Usage
docs.text:
You can capture token usage in:

  • extensions

  • Web integrations

  • Server logs (API wrappers)

  • Performance dashboards

docs.title: Token Usage Logging
docs.text: Introduced in v.0.3.2 Multimodal-Agent can automatically record token usage for monitoring, experimentation, and cost awareness.

docs.title: How It Works
docs.text:
After each LLM request (online or offline), the agent writes a single line to:

~/.multimodal_agent/usage.log

Example:

2025-01-12T10:11:31Z | model=gemini-2.5-flash | prompt=53 | response=12 | total=65

The log directory is created automatically if missing.

docs.title: Enable / Disable
docs.text:
Usage logging isn automatically record t

agent.usage_logging = False

docs.title: Custom Log Location
docs.text:
agent.usage_log_path = "/var/log/agent_tokens.log"

If the folder does not exist, the agent attempts to create it safely.

docs.title: What is Logged?
docs.text: Field Meaning timestamp UTC timestamp in ISO-8601 format (e.g., 2025-11-29T15:23:45Z). model The specific model used for the request (e.g., gpt-5.1). prompt Number of input tokens consumed (your message + system instructions). response Number of output tokens generated by the model. total Total token count: prompt + response.

docs.title: Works Everywhere
docs.text:
Token usage logging supports:

  • ask()
  • ask_with_image()
  • JSON response mode
  • offline FakeResponse mode
  • retry logic paths
  • chat mode

Logging is fully isolated from the main execution path and cannot raise exceptions.

docs.title: When To Disable
docs.text:
You may turn logging off if:

  • running automated load tests
  • storing results in your own telemetry system
  • you need a zero-logging environment

Disable:

agent.usage_logging = False

docs.title: multimodal_agent
docs.text: This module provides the main high-level interface for interacting with Gemini models using text, images, and optional RAG storage.

docs.title: Constructor
docs.text:
MultiModalAgent(
    model="gemini-2.0-flash-lite",
    enable_rag=False,
    rag_store=None,
    embedding_model="embedding-001",
)

docs.title: Methods
docs.text:
ask(question: str, response_format="text") -> AgentResponse

Send a text prompt.

resp = agent.ask("hello")
resp.text   # string
resp.usage  # token usage dict

JSON example:

resp = agent.ask("give json", response_format="json")
resp.data   # parsed dict
ask_with_image(question: str, image: Part, response_format="text") -> AgentResponse

Send a question with an image part.

chat(...)

Multi-turn conversation interface.

AgentResponse

Returned by all calls:

docs.title:
resp.text   # raw response text
resp.data   # dict for JSON mode, else None
resp.usage  # token metadata
Utilities
docs.text:
load_image_as_part(path)

Loads an image from disk and converts it into a Gemini-compatible Part.

docs.title: RAG Components
docs.text:
The agent supports plug-and-play RAG via:

  • rag_store.add_logical_message

  • rag_store.add_embedding

  • rag_store.search_similar

See docs/rag_store.md for details.
